name: Steam Engine
tagline: External combustion machine that expands steam against pistons or turbines to produce rotary or reciprocating work
problem:
Waterwheels need rivers; horses need hay. Industrial tasks — pumping mines, driving forge bellows, spinning mills — needed concentrated, schedulable power decoupled from muscle and weather. The steam engine solves this by a closed loop: boil water, expand vapour against moving boundaries, exhaust lower-pressure steam, condense or vent, and repeat.

Early engines were thermally inefficient and mechanically crude; Newcomen atmospheric engines pumped water using vacuum after steam condensation. Watt's separate condenser and later high-pressure expansions improved efficiency enough for widespread stationary power and eventually self-propelled locomotion.

The engineering obstacles are materials and safety: pressure vessels fail catastrophically; sloppy valve timing wastes steam; lubricants must survive heat and water contamination.
overview:
A reciprocating steam engine includes a boiler producing high-pressure steam, throttle and governor controlling admission, a slide or piston valve timing steam admission and exhaust to a cylinder, a piston converting pressure to linear motion, and a crankshaft with flywheel smoothing torque pulsations. Exhaust routes to a condenser (vacuum engines) or atmosphere.

Boilers are riveted or welded shells with tubes passing hot gases through water (fire-tube) or water through hot gas paths (water-tube). Feedwater must be treated to reduce scale — scale insulates tubes leading to hot spots and rupture.

Turbine steam engines (later industrial dominance for large power) use staged nozzles accelerating steam onto blades — higher speeds, fewer reciprocating parts, different bearing challenges.

Stationary engines transmitted power via flat belts on stepped pulleys to line shafts — linking to our pulley entry. The locomotive variant rigidly couples engine to wheels on rails — see steam-locomotive entry.

principles.name: Rankine cycle (idealised)
principles.explanation: Steam engines realise a heat-work cycle: heat in during boiling/superheat, work out during expansion, heat rejection during condensation — second law limits efficiency below Carnot bound set by temperature extremes.

principles.name: Cut-off and expansion
principles.explanation: Stopping steam admission early lets expanding steam do additional work — compound engines stage expansion across cylinders for efficiency.

principles.name: Boiler stress and hoop tension
principles.explanation: Pressure vessel walls see tensile hoop stress scaling with radius over thickness — why inspections and relief valves are moral necessities.

principles.name: Lubrication in wet steam
principles.explanation: Cylinder oils must resist washout; poor lubrication scores cylinders, causing blow-by and unbalanced loads.

components.name: Boiler
components.position: Firebox below or adjacent to water shell
components.madeFrom: Riveted iron plates historically; steel later
components.criticalNote: Relief valves, water level glass, blowdown — non-negotiable

components.name: Cylinder and piston
components.function: Converts steam pressure to linear force
components.position: Bolted to frame inline with crank throw
components.madeFrom: Cast iron cylinder, steel piston with rings
components.criticalNote: Keep condensate from hydraulic locking cylinder — drain cocks before start

components.name: Valve gear
components.position: Driven from eccentric or link motion
components.criticalNote: Maladjusted valves cause knocking and wasted steam

components.name: Crankshaft and flywheel
components.function: Converts reciprocation to rotation; stores kinetic energy
components.madeFrom: Forged steel shaft, cast iron flywheel
components.criticalNote: Flywheel keying must be perfect — loose keys are lethal projectiles

components.name: Governor
components.function: Limits overspeed by throttling steam or adjusting cut-off
components.criticalNote: Governor failure on unloaded engine causes runaway — engineering ethics demand redundant safeguards

rawMaterials.name: Boiler plate steel
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Riveting/welding to code; nondestructive testing modern

rawMaterials.name: Fuel
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar heat for small boilers in vacuum-adjacent demos — niche

rawMaterials.name: Feedwater
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Softening and oxygen scavenging when chemistry available

buildSteps.title: Design to pressure code
buildSteps.description: Compute wall thickness from pressure, diameter, and allowable stress; include corrosion allowance; place relief valves sized to boiler output.
buildSteps.warningNote: Amateur boilers kill — this step is legally regulated in modern nations

buildSteps.title: Fabricate and hydrotest boiler
buildSteps.description: Rivet or weld seams; X-ray or dye penetrant critical welds; hydrostatic test above working pressure with remote barriers.
buildSteps.warningNote: During test, assume lethal energy — no one in plane of shell seams

buildSteps.title: Machine cylinder and fit rings
buildSteps.description: Bore cylinder to roundness; lap if needed; cut ring gaps to allow thermal expansion without butt-scuffing.

buildSteps.title: Assemble valve gear timed to crank
buildSteps.description: Set eccentric throw relative to dead centres; verify admission edge does not occur before dead centre beyond design lead angle.
buildSteps.warningNote: Incorrect timing can slam piston into head

buildSteps.title: Steam trial with warm-up discipline
buildSteps.description: Gradually warm piping; crack drains; spin by hand; engage load slowly; monitor water level and bearing temperatures.
buildSteps.warningNote: Entanglement with belts — machine guarding is not optional ethically

history.event: Thomas Newcomen's atmospheric engine pumps water from mines using steam condensation vacuum.
James Watt patents improvements including a separate condenser, launching the efficiency trajectory toward industrial power.
High-pressure engines by Richard Trevithick enable compact locomotive prototypes.

inventors.name: James Watt
inventors.contribution: Separate condenser and foundational improvements to steam engine efficiency

inventors.name: Thomas Newcomen
inventors.contribution: Practical atmospheric steam engine for pumping

impact:
Steam power industrialised textiles, mills, shipping (steamships), and railways. It taught thermodynamics to engineers and created the safety culture around pressure vessels.

Modern grids still use steam turbines — the working fluid changed scale, not conceptual role.
